title: Icy Tangy Argentinian Sugars Bake
description: A light and tangy Argentinian dessert featuring sugars, traditionally cooked in a clay pot. Perfect for warm evenings or as a treat during festive gatherings.
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine granulated sugar, confectioners' sugar, and lemon juice. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves.
Add the water to the saucepan and stir to combine.
In a small bowl, whisk together cornstarch and a pinch of salt. Add the cornstarch mixture to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Continue to cook the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the butter and vanilla extract. Saffron threads may be added for color and flavor if desired.
Pour the mixture into individual serving dishes or a large clay pot.
Cover the dishes or pot with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to chill and set.
Serve the Icy Tangy Argentinian Sugars Bake chilled, garnished with additional confectioners' sugar if desired.
To serve, dip individual servings in hot water for 30 seconds to loosen and unmold.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is served unmolded and garnished with a sprinkle of confectioners' sugar and a few saffron threads.
To unmold, dip individual servings in hot water for 30 seconds and invert onto plates or serving dishes.
